item.title: Re: Rain, Rain Go Away
item.content:encoded: For anyone who doesn't know what the italicized, bolded text is, it's the beginning of the song "Spanish Sahara" by Foals.

item.title: Re: Rain, Rain Go Away
item.content:encoded:
*facepalms*

I didn't get it. I came here because I didn't get it. I don't know what I was expecting.

Sigh. Just sigh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that this is presented in far too straightforward of a manner. You've got a mysterious and enigmatic object but then simultaneously the reader is being handheld through the story. In my own SCP-703, I gave the object simple but easily identifiable personality traits. The object was less a vehicle through which the story was told and more the protagonist of the story. I don't really feel anything from the cloud, it's delivering the weekly mystery box subscription. It's delivering story but doesn't feel like a part of it. That's why this doesn't work right now. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I'm probably just dumb and/or tired, but other than that it has something to do with the September 11 attacks (I think?), I have no clue what's going on here. No-vote for now. Perhaps I'll try to come back and make heads or tails of it again later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, I might have been misinformed then. It just stuck out to me.
I apologize.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "White" is actually the most clinical way of saying that, as opposed to Caucasian (based on Caucasoid), which is an outdated, ill-defined, and improper taxon. The author could also specify the exact ethnic group the children belong to, but given the circumstances of the article, how would researchers in-universe identify this if it's just a photograph?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2 white children

"white" is not clinical tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You seem to be very skilled at "show, don't tell" but it seemed rather bland for me. I'm not really into very short SCPs but nonetheless, it's a fair attempt at a simple SCP but I'll hold off on voting for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually had to read through this twice. I think you could absolutely do without the package types and measurements, as those hinder the progress to the next section.

Overall, I'm a bit iffy on it. I think this could have been executed a bit clearer, but from what I understand this is a fairly solid concept. I'm willing to give it an upvote on that, but I hesitate to do so. It's good, but it isn't great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've read all three of your works thus far, and I have to say: this is the best one yet. It looks like you've figured out "show, don't tell".

As I mentioned in chat, there are still a couple of places where I think this needs improvement, but other than that, I like this. +1

item.title: Rain, Rain Go Away
item.content:encoded:
So I walked through to the haze
And a million dirty waves
Now I see you lying there
Like a lilo losing air, air
Black rocks and shoreline sand
Still dead summer I cannot bear
And I wipe the sand from my arms
The Spanish Sahara
The place that you'd wanna
Leave the horror here

Image creds: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cahokia_1.jpg

A short SCP, which my friends know, is not like me at all. Inspiration occurred after my grandpa pointed outside and asked how to send messages through a cloud.

Thanks to KnightAlaska, FloppyPhoenix, DStarsthy, Clem, and Rura for peepin at it.
